records.ety: [OE. popet, OF. poupette; akin to F. poupe a doll, probably from L. puppa, pupa, a girl, doll, puppet. Cf. Poupeton, Pupa, Pupil, Puppy.]

defs: A small image in the human form; a doll.
A similar figure moved by the hand or by a wire in a mock drama; a marionette; a wooden actor in a play.
quotes: At the pipes of some carved organ move, The gilded puppets dance.

defs: One controlled in his action by the will of another; a tool; -- so used in contempt.
The upright support for the bearing of the spindle in a lathe.
